_comment: SLICE-7 MOVE EXECUTED (2026-07-01): counterweight-triage.md RETIRED from requiredCommandFragments (census INLINE); RCF now [autonomous-trigger.md] (genuine DEPTH). PROVEN FROM THE EXISTING COMMITTED CAPTURE (no fresh capture needed — the floor got strictly weaker and autonomous-trigger.md was GENUINELY opened by the representative run per the committed finding + observed 'All declared claims met'; SKILL.md unchanged). --- SLICE-7 CENSUS RECONCILIATION (2026-07-01) — SUPERSEDES THE CAPTURE-ONLY FRAMING BELOW WHERE IT CONTRADICTS census.json. Reconciled verdict: counterweight-triage.md=MOVE (census INLINE). The default/autonomous run GENUINELY opened it, but for the DUP-inlined four-bin prose (SKILL.md:37), not the opt-in `## Structured Findings` schema — so 'opened → PROVEN keep' below is the flagged method error. Census 'inline the schema/template' → RCF floor retires (capture-gated), doc stays DECLARED for on-demand authoring. Full adversarial (analyze→verify) resolution + capture-gated queue: charness-artifacts/reference-compaction/slice7-census-reconciliation.md. classTag/RCF are intentionally NOT flipped here: claim_fidelity_lib forbids DUP/INLINE-tagging a live RCF floor, so the tag/RCF flip rides with the capture-gated MOVE (no substance judge + no retained stream ⇒ each MOVE needs a fresh ask-before-run capture). --- ORIGINAL SPEC COMMENT: Claim-fidelity spec for the critique skill, AUTONOMOUS scenario (default fixture): does a bare `/charness:critique` run honor its own routing? critique is a self-activator — with no pending artifact it does NOT ask first but follows references/autonomous-trigger.md to infer a bounded target from repo state (SKILL.md 42-45; Bootstrap: 'For no-argument slash-command use, run the autonomous trigger scan first'). The deterministic RCF proves only that the run FOLLOWED its path: autonomous-trigger.md (it ran the inference scan = it engaged repo state to pick a target) and counterweight-triage.md (it triaged concerns into the four bins, step 5). Which of the five target references opens is NON-DETERMINISTIC (depends on what the scan infers is pending), so each stays on-demand and is not hard-matched — pinning a specific target here would be matcher theater. The QUALITY of the inference (did it read the right change surface, pick a sensible target) is the Cautilus recommendation / LLM-judge signal scored at live capture, not a deterministic fragment (methodology two-signal model). The pinned-target counterpart is decision.spec.json. thresholds omitted until a first baseline capture sets a runtime budget. SLICE 7 (2026-07-01) CAPTURE — floor PROVEN, #410 drop REFUTED. The bare /charness:critique run self-activated into a full multi-angle+counterweight review and GENUINELY OPENED BOTH RCF docs — autonomous-trigger.md (Read) and counterweight-triage.md (Read + `cat` + `sed`) — coverage 4/12, outcome=passed, emitting the four-bin triage (counterweight/Over-Worry/Act Before Ship/Valid but Defer). So counterweight-triage.md is load-bearing for the autonomous scenario and #410's RCF-drop does NOT apply. No SKILL.md lift needed (four-bin inlined at SKILL.md:37; ~195 ceiling untouched). thresholds.max_duration_ms set from this passing baseline (621849ms, ~2x) — the autonomous path spawns 3 angle subagents + a counterweight pass, so it is an expensive but faithful run.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Documents optional adapter configuration; consulted only when the repo declares .agents/critique-adapter.yaml or needs to configure packet sections.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: when configuring critique adapter behavior, setting reviewer tiers, or understanding adapter lookup order and field semantics

angle-selection.md.rationale: Provides background on the five anchors and angle selection principles; the target reference's Anchor Angle Distribution is primary, this is supplementary.
angle-selection.md.trigger: when understanding the anchor lineup, selecting contrasting angles, or reviewing angle weighting for non-standard change surfaces

autonomous-trigger.md.rationale: In the autonomous (no-argument) scenario the run MUST scan repo state to infer a bounded target: SKILL.md routes 'follow references/autonomous-trigger.md' and the Bootstrap mandates the autonomous-trigger scan for no-argument slash-command use, so a bare run that skips it did not follow its own self-activation path.

cadence.md.rationale: Governs escalation ladder and review-unit scoping before critique is invoked; consulted by parent workflows, not by the representative run itself.
cadence.md.trigger: when deciding whether to invoke standalone critique or use inline critique, or when defining review unit boundaries for task-completing work

code-critique.md.rationale: Routes to this target reference only when change type is code/diff; shapes angle distribution and counterweight bins for code review.
code-critique.md.trigger: when the pending change is concrete code under review (code critique, PR critique, commit critique, pre-merge review)

confirmed-input-over-anchoring.md.rationale: Provides an optional angle lens for specialized anti-anchoring review; consulted only when the condition (confirmed input + system variance) is present.
confirmed-input-over-anchoring.md.trigger: when a pending change locks a value from user confirmation, issue framing, or prior session memory, and the repo varies on a known axis

counterweight-triage.md.rationale: RETIRED from requiredCommandFragments (reference-compaction Slice-7 reconciliation, 2026-07-01): census INLINE — the four-bin triage the runs actually apply is inlined verbatim at SKILL.md:37; the `## Structured Findings` schema is opt-in + validator-gated (validate_critique_artifacts.py) and was NOT opened-for-schema by any representative run, so the doc-open was not a load-bearing floor. classTag INLINE; stays DECLARED for on-demand Structured-Findings authoring, but is no longer a re-read floor. Retiring it also removes this spec's matcher-theater pass where applicable (a subagent-prompt name-mention satisfied the old floor, #415).

premortem-decision.md.rationale: Routes to this target reference only when change type is a decision; shapes angle distribution and counterweight bins for that specific change class.
premortem-decision.md.trigger: when the pending change is a decision lock-in without committed code, release, or rename (decision premortem, design lock-in, workflow contract change)

prepare-packet.md.rationale: Conditional mechanism fired by adapter configuration; when adapter declares sections, bootstrap runs the prepare runner before spawning reviewers.
prepare-packet.md.trigger: when the repo's .agents/critique-adapter.yaml declares ≥1 packet_sections entry, preparing a deterministic packet for fresh-eye reviewers

release-critique.md.rationale: Routes to this target reference only when change type is release; shapes angle distribution, counterweight bins, and surface-lock inventory for release lock-in.
release-critique.md.trigger: when the pending change is a release surface about to commit (release critique, pre-release review, before cutting tag or bumping version)

rename-critique.md.rationale: Routes to this target reference only when change type is rename/deletion; shapes angle distribution, counterweight bins, and first-reader probe for rename lock-in.
rename-critique.md.trigger: when the pending change moves, renames, or removes a named concept across multiple sites (rename critique, deletion review, skill/directory/module rename)

spec-critique.md.rationale: Routes to this target reference only when change type is spec/design; shapes angle distribution, counterweight bins, and coherence checks for spec lock-in.
spec-critique.md.trigger: when the pending change is a spec or design contract about to gate implementation (spec critique, spec premortem, pre-impl review)
